failure_modes.text: A vivid case can overpower base rates and make a category feel more probable than the surrounding distribution supports.
failure_modes.source_quote: The personality description is vivid and salient, overriding the cold, statistical logic.
failure_modes.mitigation: Check the base rate before the story: for any vivid case that feels diagnostic, look up how frequently the category actually occurs in the relevant reference class.

failure_modes.text: Experts can force a new problem into an old pattern and end up with disastrously wrong solutions.
failure_modes.source_quote: This incorrect pattern recognition risks disastrously wrong solutions.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test pattern fit explicitly: list the structural features the new case shares with the template and at least one it does not, then judge whether the mismatch is material.

failure_modes.text: Smart teams can become especially vulnerable when they construct elegant narratives that hide their own bias blind spot.
failure_modes.source_quote: The more cognitively sophisticated a person is, the better they are at constructing narratives and rationalizing data to support their preconceived beliefs
failure_modes.mitigation: Assign a designated base-rate advocate: when the team's narrative is compelling, one member's job is to surface the statistical denominator and test the story against it.

premortem_questions.text: What would you have to believe for the favored representative story to be truer than the base-rate evidence?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

premortem_questions.text: Is the worst case bad enough if this resemblance-based judgment is wrong?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.text: Why might my belief not be true?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Why might my belief not be true?

heuristics.text: Force an outside-view pass with reference classes or base rates before acting on an intuitive category match.
Separate independent judgments before discussion so the first representative story does not anchor the whole group.
heuristics.source_quote: obtain separate, independent judgments on the issue before any discussion takes place

heuristics.text: Challenge the favored frame with thesis, antithesis, and synthesis instead of letting the first pattern match become the decision.
heuristics.source_quote: Adopt the dialectic standard: require every idea or hypothesis ... to be met with its antithesis

curation_notes.summary: The source is strongest on vivid-case substitution, base-rate correction, and false pattern-recognition risk.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor anti-bias sprawl beyond the raw source's clearer base-rate, outside-view, and structured-challenge material.
open_questions: The third failure mode compresses the source's warning about smart rationalizers and bias blind spot into one reusable intervention chunk.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on confirmation-bias, WYSIATI-style story completion, and framing effects.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is strongest on System 2, multidisciplinary models, and explicit base-rate analysis.
structured_tensions: The source is strongest on base-rates and confirmation-bias pressure, but it does not present a neat explicit structured tension block in current-vocabulary form.
